subject: SCIENCE
title: Five Sense Helpers
objective: Connect primary sense organs with observations while recognizing senses work together.
introduction: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Five Sense Helpers”.
A key word used in the lesson “Five Sense Helpers”.
A key word used in the lesson “Five Sense Helpers”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Taste works without smell in every way

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Which organ primarily helps detect sound?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. eyes and tongue do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Five Sense Helpers”?

options.text: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.
Taste works without smell in every way

items.explanation: The correct response is “Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.

options.text: Taste works without smell in every way

items.explanation: The correct response is “eyes—light and color” because it follows the lesson explanation: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: Taste works without smell in every way

items.explanation: The correct response is “Taste works without smell in every way” because it follows the lesson explanation: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.

items.prompt: Which organ primarily helps detect sound?
items.explanation: The correct response is “ears” because it follows the lesson explanation: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Taste works without smell in every way

items.explanation: The correct response is “skin—touch” because it follows the lesson explanation: Eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin are primary helpers, and senses often work together.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Five Sense Helpers; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Five Sense Helpers.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
